assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In plan.md Step 4 'Understand the Code', the plan explicitly lists inspecting: 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (line 76), 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' (line 77), and references 'advisory/endpoints/get.rs' (line 82: 'Existing GET handler in advisory/endpoints/get.rs -- understand handler signature and flow'). It also references 'advisory/model/summary.rs' in the sibling analysis (line 94). That covers at least 3 of the 4 listed files.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6 lists exactly 6 files: 3 to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs) and 3 to create (advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs). Step 9 'Scope containment' (lines 171-181) explicitly verifies all changed files are in the Files to Modify/Create lists. No unrelated files are modified.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 (lines 207-215) shows the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' (Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'advisory'), and the footer contains 'Implements TC-9201'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 line 217 explicitly states: "With trailer: `--trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'`"

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md line 9 states 'Branch Name: TC-9201'. Step 5 (lines 128-132) shows 'git checkout main' and 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. Step 10 line 222 shows 'git push -u origin TC-9201' and 'gh pr create --base main'. The Target Branch 'main' is referenced at line 7 and in the PR creation command.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md clearly identifies both patterns. Under 'Error handling' (lines 47-49): 'All service and handler errors use Result<T, AppError>' and '.context() wrapping on every fallible operation' and 'AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs'. Under 'Module structure' (lines 17-19): 'Every domain module under modules/fundamental/src/ follows the pattern: model/ + service/ + endpoints/' and 'Siblings: sbom/, advisory/, package/ all follow this structure identically'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md line 7 states 'Target Branch: main'. Step 1 (line 31) lists 'Target Branch: main' as a required section present in the task description. Step 5 shows 'git checkout main' before creating the feature branch. Step 10 line 222 shows '--base main' in the PR creation command.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1.5 'Verify Description Integrity' (lines 46-55) explicitly describes: 'Would fetch issue comments via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201) and search for comments starting with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:'. Under 'If no digest comment found (backward compatibility)': 'Log warning and proceed normally: "No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced."'
